title: Purged-forward ordered pipeline instead of shuffled grouped CV
summary: The current best code still uses shuffled grouped CV and several transductive transforms built on train+test together.
rationale: The current best code still uses shuffled grouped CV and several transductive transforms built on train+test together. Public guidance on purging/embargo and CatBoost ordered statistics suggests small CV gains can disappear when future-adjacent rows or future category information leak into training.
implementation_hint: Create event-ordered folds by Year/Race with a 1-race embargo, recompute all category frequencies/interaction counts/adversarial weights inside each fold from train-slice only, and test a CatBoost branch with rows sorted chronologically and `has_time=True` plus ordered CTRs only.
expected_effect: Likely lowers optimistic local CV slightly but should improve robustness and may reveal real leaderboard-positive changes that are currently hidden by leakage-friendly validation.
risk: Can look worse on current CV even if it is more correct; fold-safe recomputation is slower and removes some of the apparent benefit from transductive stats.
